sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to pickled cucumbers (in some countries cucumber pickles) intended for direct consumption which are: (a) prepared with cucumbers as the predominant ingredient; (b) prepared from desalted cured cucumbers, fermented naturally or by controlled fermentation, or from fresh cucumbers which are acidulated; or (c) preserved through natural or control fermentation or added acidulants and may be further preserved by pasteurization with heat, by other physical means or by chemical preservatives. In some countries, the word “cucumbers” is associated with large size fruit whereas the word “gherkin” (in French “cornichons”) refers to small fruit. Irrespective of customary national trade practice these products are included in this standard. This standard does not cover finely chopped products called relish.

sections.title: Product definition
sections.text: Picked cucumbers is the product: (a) prepared from clean, sound cucumbers of cultivars conforming to the characteristics of Cucumis sativus L.; (b) that may or may not be peeled and may or may not have seeds removed; (c) packed with or without a suitable liquid packing medium and seasoning ingredients appropriate to the product; and (d) is preserved in an appropriate manner before or after the container is closed – such preservation to include acidulation to a pH of 4.6 or less either by natural or controlled fermentation or addition of a vinegar or an edible acid, and may also include heat pasteurization, refrigeration or a chemical preservative.

sections.text:
(a) Whole – cucumbers with a maximum diameter of 54  mm. In containers larger than 4  litres, the cucumbers may have a maximum diameter of 65  mm. Whole cucumbers of this style may be designated as “gherkin” when they are not larger than 27 mm in diameter.
(b) Whole curved – whole cucumbers with a maximum diameter of 54 mm and curved at least 35°.
(c) Halves – cucumbers divided lengthwise into halves.
(d) Finger cut, sliced lengthwise or spears – cucumbers cut lengthwise into sections of approximately equal size.
(e) Ring cut or chunks – cucumbers cut at right angles to the longitudinal axis having a thickness from 10 to 40 mm and a maximum diameter of 54 mm.
(f) Slices or cross cuts or chips – cucumbers cut at right angles to the longitudinal axis having a thickness of not more than 10 mm and a maximum diameter of 54 mm.
(g) Strips (“Asier”) – large cucumbers, peeled, divided lengthwise. The prepared halves are cut at right angle to the longitudinal direction into strips of approximately 10 mm width.

sections.title: Other styles
sections.retrieval_text:
OTHER STYLES
number=2.4
level=2
Any other presentation of the product shall be permitted provided that the product:
(a) is sufficiently distinctive from other forms of presentation laid down in this standard;
(b) meets all relevant requirements of this standard, including requirements relating to limitations on defects, drained weight, and any other requirements in this standard which are applicable to that style in the standard which most closely resembles the style or styles intended to be provided for under this provision;
(c) is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.

sections.title: Size uniformity
sections.text:
(a) Whole style: spears or sliced lengthwise
80 percent or more, by count, of the cucumbers shall meet the following requirements:
Length – the length of the longest unit shall not exceed that of the shortest unit by more than 50 percent.
Diameter – the diameter of the largest unit shall not exceed that of the smallest unit by more than 50 percent.
These requirements do not apply to cucumbers packed in containers of over 4 litres.
(b) Ring cut; slices; cross cuts
80 percent or more by weight of units having the most uniform size meet the following requirement for individual containers or sample units.
Diameter – the diameter of the largest unit shall not exceed that of the smallest unit by more than 50 percent.

sections.title: Definition of defects
sections.text: (a) Curved cucumbers – means whole cucumbers that are curved at an angle of 35° when measured as illustrated in Annex I. (b) Misshaped cucumbers – means whole cucumbers, nubbins, and other deformed cucumbers as illustrated in Annex I. (c) Blemished – means affected to a degree that materially detracts from appearance and edibility by discolouration, scars, scratches, skin breaks or other similar imperfections. (d) Mechanical damage – means crushed or broken units. (e) Stem – means any stalk longer than 15 mm. (f) Poor texture – excessively shrivelled, very soft or flabby or units with very large seeds. (g) Off colour – units that vary markedly from the colour typical of the variety and type of pack. (h) Hollow centres – whole cucumbers in which the internal cavity is large or ring cuts and slices in which a substantial portion of the centre is missing. (i) Grit, sand or silt – means any mineral impurities, whether in the liquid packing medium or imbedded in the skin or flesh of the cucumbers that affect the edibility.

sections.text:
VISUAL AID ILLUSTRATING CURVED AND MISSHAPEN CUCUMBERS
(a) Curved cucumber. A curved cucumber is one that is curved at an angle of 35 degrees or more, when measured as illustrated.
(b) Misshapen cucumbers. Misshapen cucumbers include crooked, nubbins, and otherwise misshapen cucumbers. A nubbin cucumber is one that is not cylindrical in form, is short and stubby, or is not well developed. Nubbins and otherwise misshapen cucumbers are similar to the following illustrations:

sentences.original: This standard applies to pickled cucumbers (in some countries cucumber pickles) intended for direct consumption which are: (a) prepared with cucumbers as the predominant ingredient; (b) prepared from desalted cured cucumbers, fermented naturally or by controlled fermentation, or from fresh cucumbers which are acidulated; or (c) preserved through natural or control fermentation or added acidulants and may be further preserved by pasteurization with heat, by other physical means or by chemical preservatives.

sentences.original: Picked cucumbers is the product: (a) prepared from clean, sound cucumbers of cultivars conforming to the characteristics of Cucumis sativus L.; (b) that may or may not be peeled and may or may not have seeds removed; (c) packed with or without a suitable liquid packing medium and seasoning ingredients appropriate to the product; and (d) is preserved in an appropriate manner before or after the container is closed – such preservation to include acidulation to a pH of 4.6 or less either by natural or controlled fermentation or addition of a vinegar or an edible acid, and may also include heat pasteurization, refrigeration or a chemical preservative.

classification.sentence: Picked cucumbers is the product: (a) prepared from clean, sound cucumbers of cultivars conforming to the characteristics of Cucumis sativus L.; (b) that may or may not be peeled and may or may not have seeds removed; (c) packed with or without a suitable liquid packing medium and seasoning ingredients appropriate to the product; and (d) is preserved in an appropriate manner before or after the container is closed – such preservation to include acidulation to a pH of 4.6 or less either by natural or controlled fermentation or addition of a vinegar or an edible acid, and may also include heat pasteurization, refrigeration or a chemical preservative.
classification.function: Defines product characteristics by enumerated conditions
classification.pattern: Product defined by multiple enumerated characteristics (a) to (d)

characteristics.a: prepared from clean, sound cucumbers of cultivars conforming to the characteristics of Cucumis sativus L.
characteristics.b: may or may not be peeled and may or may not have seeds removed
characteristics.c: packed with or without a suitable liquid packing medium and seasoning ingredients appropriate to the product
characteristics.d: preserved in an appropriate manner before or after the container is closed including acidulation to pH 4.6 or less by natural or controlled fermentation or addition of vinegar or edible acid, may include heat pasteurization, refrigeration or chemical preservative

classification.justification: Sentence sets multiple conditions defining the product.

pages.summary: Página contém título principal do documento com identificador e histórico de adoção e emendas, sem subseções. Apenas um nó estrutural nível 1 para o título principal.

A página contém um cabeçalho repetitivo, nenhum título principal do documento identificado, e um único item estrutural de nível 1 com título '2022 Amendment' seguido do texto explicativo. A estrutura é simples e clara para uso em RAG.

A página apresenta a norma CXS 115-1981 como cabeçalho repetitivo, sem título principal do documento. Possui uma estrutura clara de seções e subseções numeradas: SCOPE (1), DESCRIPTION (2) com subseções 2.1 Product definition e 2.2 Types and kinds of pack, esta última contendo três subsubseções 2.2.1, 2.2.2 e 2.2.3. Textos explicativos estão associados corretamente a seus respectivos títulos. Algumas seções continuam para a próxima página, indicando continuidade para RAG.

A página contém a seção principal 2.3 Styles com texto introdutório e detalhamento dos estilos apresentados em formato de lista. A seção 2.3 inicia na página anterior e continua nesta, sem avançar para a próxima página. O cabeçalho da norma CXS 115-1981 está presente, sem título principal de documento.

Página contém a seção 2.4 que inicia na página anterior e continua aqui, e a seção 3 com subtítulos e subsubtítulos detalhados de composição e qualidade. Estrutura hierárquica respeitada, textos associados corretamente para RAG.

Página 6 contém as subseções 3.2.5 e 3.2.6 dentro da seção 3.2, com definições detalhadas de defeitos e limites permitidos. A seção 3.2.5 continua da página anterior. Textos associados corretamente aos títulos, sem rodapé incluído.

A página 7 contém a continuação da seção 3, com subseções 3.2.7, 3.3 e 3.4, seguidos da seção 4 FOOD ADDITIVES e suas subseções 4.1 a 4.5. Textos associados a cada título estão adequados, e a seção 4 é marcada como continua para a próxima página devido ao seu conteúdo extenso.

Esta página contém as seções 4.6 e 4.7 continuando da página anterior, seguidas das seções 5 a 8. A seção 4.6 está marcada como continuação da página anterior e continua para a próxima página. A hierarquia foi mapeada até nível 3, refletindo os títulos numerados e o texto associado. Os textos foram corretamente associados aos seus respectivos nós estruturais, sem inclusão do cabeçalho repetitivo. As seções estruturais foram identificadas por seus números e títulos em caixa alta ou destacados.

Na página 9 do documento ef4872d3-84bf-4dcf-ac3a-fa9f913e423d, foram identificadas as seções 8 (com subseções 8.1.2, 8.1.3, 8.2) e 9 (título em caixa alta). Os títulos numerados e em caixa alta foram usados para hierarquização. Os textos internos foram corretamente associados a seus respectivos nós e não há indicações de continuidade entre páginas para estas seções nesta página.

Identificado título principal estrutural 'ANNEX I' em nível 1. Texto associado à seção explica definições visuais de pepinos curvos e deformados. Indicada continuidade para a próxima página pois o conteúdo visual provavelmente segue.
A página apresenta um título de nível 1 'NOTES' seguido por lista numerada de referências bibliográficas. Não há indicações de continuidade com a página anterior ou seguinte.
